comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes; all clean (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied):

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). This child only READS the gate/set; the reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. Verified `managed_refs` does not yet exist in the tree (search: no matches) — parent builds it. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent => REMOVE/CLEAR iff in managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, no inbound-remove dual. ba2c ("CLEAR only when Jira parent in managed_refs, else ADOPT") matches 908c ("REMOVE only when in managed_refs, else adopt") and parent's `should_propagate_removal` definition. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — ba2c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate existing; the parent-child hierarchy (b4d4 builds it; both children carry parent_id b4d4) supplies the ordering, and parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. No undeclared gap. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — ba2c touches the PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, the `emit_detach_clear` branch); sibling 908c touches the LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE). Disjoint code paths verified: the `emit_detach_clear=True` opt-in (outbound_differ.py:542) and the un-gated CLEAR branch (outbound_differ.py:400-414) match ba2c's root cause exactly. Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — ba2c and 908c are independent consumers (parent vs links); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children rely on same-pass inbound suppression (ba2c: replace the CLEAR-time suppression of inbound parent adoption with adoption when the gate denies; 908c: link-ADD echo suppression) on DISJOINT ref kinds (parent vs links). The `_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD` map already carries parent→parent_id (inbound_differ.py:578-581). No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — ba2c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog" in its Design; confirmed b4d4 is the parent and 908c cites the same alias. ba2c's own alias is tan-elbow-mica. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 / T11 (Data-migration & backfill safety): PASS. The "migration" is a lazy, per-ticket in-memory reseed in the reducer (seed managed_refs from current deps + parent_id on first reduce/restore where absent), not a schema DDL or bulk backfill transaction. It is idempotent (only seeds on absence; strictly monotonic thereafter), needs no batching (per-ticket cardinality is tens, applied on the normal reduce path), has an explicit back-out (disable the fold → gate degrades to a safe no-op, additive-only), and is forward-compatible (older clones preserve-and-ignore; fsck WARNs on newer state). No downtime/dual-write/rollback hazards apply to this event-sourced reseed.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

4-bucket audit of parent SC/AC, each flagged absence cross-checked against the WHOLE roster:

- SC1 (provider-agnostic primitive consumed by BOTH parent + link removal, no Jira logic in gate): FULLY (joint) — ba2c consumes the gate on the PARENT CLEAR path (CLEAR emitted only when Jira parent ∈ managed_refs; never-managed parent ADOPTED), 908c on the LINK path. Both halves present; ba2c AC4 affirms "parent + link removal share the same gate helper (no duplicated provenance logic)."
- AC1 (reducer folds managed_refs: monotonic, sorted, never reduced by UNLINK): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope retains "the reducer managed_refs field + compaction preservation + byte-parity" as its OWN in-scope primitive work; consumers only READ the set. Not delegated to children → not a child-attributable gap.
- AC2 (absent managed_refs → seed from deps+parent_id; pre-feature-compacted test): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope retains "migration-seeding."
- AC3 (survives compact_ticket; compact-then-reconcile test): COVERED behaviorally on consumer paths — ba2c AC3 "incl. across a compaction boundary" (parent kind) + 908c AC5 "survives a compaction boundary" (link kind); restore mechanism (process_snapshot) is parent-side.
- AC4 (SNAPSHOT byte-parity / deterministic serialization): STRUCTURAL — parent-owned serialization.
- AC5 (shared should_propagate_removal exists, provider-agnostic, defaults empty→False, unit-tested REMOVE-vs-ADOPT): gate existence + its own unit tests are STRUCTURAL (parent Scope: "the shared provider-agnostic gate helper with its own unit tests"); the REMOVE-vs-ADOPT DECISION is additionally exercised jointly through consumers — ba2c (managed parent → CLEAR; never-managed parent → ADOPT, regression test) + 908c (managed link → REMOVE; never-managed → ADOPT). Covered.
- AC6 (operator-facing regression: unlink a managed ref, two reconcile passes, stays removed): COVERED (joint) — 908c AC5 "local-unlink of a managed link emits an outbound remove and is not re-added inbound" is the churn-fixed regression for the LINK kind; ba2c covers the PARENT kind (human-set parent adopted not cleared; managed parent CLEAR converges).
- AC7 (docs/ documents pattern + future reclamation hook as template): STRUCTURAL — parent Scope retains "docs establishing the reusable pattern."

Absence cross-check: every parent criterion is either (a) jointly covered by the two named CONSUMERS (SC1, AC3 durability, AC5 decision, AC6 regression) or (b) parent-owned in-scope primitive-build work the Scope deliberately retains rather than delegating (AC1, AC2, AC4, gate unit-tests, AC7 docs). This container is a primitive-building story that carves reducer/seeding/byte-parity/gate-helper/docs into its OWN scope and tracks only the two consumer integrations as children. THIS child (parent-CLEAR consumer) fully covers its half; sibling 908c covers the link half. No uncovered parent criterion is attributable to the children.

Result: G3 CLEAN (empty findings).

[G4 child-consistency audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs WHOLE roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes (high-confidence + benign-reading filter); all CLEAN, verified against code:

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). Confirmed via search: `managed_refs` has zero matches in the tree → parent builds it; this child only READS the gate/set. Reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent ⇒ REMOVE/CLEAR iff ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, no inbound-remove dual. ba2c ("CLEAR only when Jira parent ∈ managed_refs, else ADOPT") matches 908c ("REMOVE iff ∈ managed_refs, else adopt") and parent's `should_propagate_removal` definition. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — ba2c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate existing; the parent-child hierarchy (both children carry parent_id b4d4, the primitive builder) supplies ordering, and parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. No undeclared gap. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — ba2c touches the PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, the `emit_detach_clear` branch; verified outbound_differ.py:400-414, `result["parent"] = None`); sibling 908c touches the LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE). Disjoint code paths confirmed. Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic (ba2c AC4: "no duplicated provenance logic"). CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — ba2c and 908c are independent consumers (parent vs links); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children extend same-pass inbound suppression, but on DISJOINT keys: ba2c uses the scalar `fields`/`parent`→`parent_id` path (_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD, inbound_differ.py:578-581,633-644); 908c uses the link-add echo set `link_add_keys` (inbound_differ.py:599-601,619-623). Verified disjoint. No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — ba2c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog" in its Design; confirmed b4d4's alias IS safe-luge-nog and 908c cites the same. ba2c's own alias is tan-elbow-mica. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

NON-G4 note (within-child / G2-G6-class, NOT a cross-child contradiction with ba2c): the Jira "Blocks" type collapses both `blocks` and `depends_on` logical kinds direction-agnostically, while parent `managed_refs` keys on direction-distinguished `(kind, target)`. This affects sibling 908c's link-REMOVE gate lookup, is already flagged on 908c and absorbed as a parent G6 finding, and does NOT contradict ba2c (parent ref is single-direction `(parent, target)`, unambiguous). Not a cross-child interaction defect.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

All four acceptance criteria are met and the reported bug is resolved. Evidence grounded in tool output follows.

---

### AC1 — Parent CLEAR emitted ONLY when Jira parent is in `managed_refs`

`outbound_differ.py:629–656` handles the `field_name == "parent"` branch in `_diff_fields`. When `local_val` is falsy (detached) and `jira_parent_key` is set, it calls `_parent_clear_is_managed(jira_parent_key, ticket, binding_store)` (defined at lines 507–526). If that returns False, it `continue`s — the CLEAR is skipped and NOT emitted. Only a truthy return (managed parent) allows `changed[field_name] = local_val` to run.

`_parent_clear_is_managed` (lines 507–526) maps the Jira key back to a local id via `binding_store.get_local_id`, then delegates to `should_propagate_removal("parent", parent_local_id, ticket)` imported from `rebar.reducer._managed_refs`. Both fail-open on an unresolvable key (returns `False` → no clobber). The old un-gated `result["parent"] = None` sentinel at line 414 is still emitted by `_map_local_to_jira_fields`, but the gate in `_diff_fields` prevents it from ever reaching the mutation output unless the managed-ref check passes.

---

### AC2 — Human-set Jira parent on never-managed ticket is ADOPTED inbound, not cleared (regression test)

`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_parent_detach_sync.py:153–194` — `test_unmanaged_jira_parent_is_not_cleared`:
- Ticket has `parent_id: None`, `managed_refs: []` (never managed any parent).
- Jira snapshot has `"parent": {"key": "PROJ-EPIC"}` (human-set).
- Asserts `not parent_muts` — zero parent mutations emitted; the human's Jira parent is preserved for inbound ADOPT.

This is exactly the regression scenario described in the ticket's Reproduction Steps.

---

### AC3 — Genuinely-detached managed parent still emits CLEAR and converges, incl. across a compaction boundary

Two tests cover this:

1. `test_parent_detach_sync.py:60–107` — `test_detach_emits_outbound_parent_clear`: ticket has `managed_refs: [["parent", "local-epic"]]` and `parent_id: None`. Asserts a parent mutation is emitted and its value is falsy (the CLEAR). This is the "drum-ruler-suit behavior preserved" check.

2. `tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py:129–148` — `test_managed_refs_survive_compaction_roundtrip`: reduces a log with a CREATE (parent_id `epic-z`) + LINK event, verifies `("parent","epic-z")` is in `managed_refs`, then writes the compiled state as a SNAPSHOT (simulating `compact_ticket`) and re-reduces from the snapshot only — asserts `managed_refs` is restored verbatim. Since the gate reads `managed_refs` from compiled state, a post-compaction detach still sees the parent as managed and propagates the CLEAR.

---

### AC4 — Parent + link removal share the same gate helper (no duplicated provenance logic)

Both consumers import and call the same single function from the same module:

- **Parent path** (`_parent_clear_is_managed`, `outbound_differ.py:518`):
  `from rebar.reducer._managed_refs import should_propagate_removal`
  Called as `should_propagate_removal("parent", parent_local_id, ticket)`.

- **Link path** (`_diff_link_removals`, `outbound_differ.py:1126`):
  `from rebar.reducer._managed_refs import should_propagate_removal`
  Called as `should_propagate_removal(relation, local_target, ticket)`.

The shared helper lives in `src/rebar/reducer/_managed_refs.py:139–152` — a single, provider-agnostic function. No duplicated provenance logic.

---

### Bug resolution

The original defect (un-gated CLEAR clobbers a human-set Jira parent) no longer reproduces: the `result["parent"] = None` sentinel from `_map_local_to_jira_fields` is only acted upon in `_diff_fields` when `_parent_clear_is_managed` returns True (i.e., `(parent, local_id)` is in `managed_refs`). A ticket with `managed_refs: []` and a Jira-side parent emits no outbound mutation, leaving the parent for inbound adoption — restoring the prior expected behavior.

**Verdict: PASS** — every acceptance criterion is satisfied by concrete, grounded implementation in the repository.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Ticket T is locally parentless (parent_id None) and bound to Jira.
2. A human sets an epic/parent P on T directly in Jira.
3. Run a reconcile pass.

**Expected:** local ADOPTS P (the prior behavior), since rebar never managed a parent for T.
**Actual (after PR #54 / drum-ruler-suit):** outbound emits a parent CLEAR unconditionally (local detached + Jira has a parent), DELETING the human's parent and suppressing inbound adoption. The un-gated CLEAR inverts the old adopt behavior into a clobber.

## Root Cause

The parent CLEAR added in PR #54 (`outbound_differ._map_local_to_jira_fields`, the `emit_detach_clear` branch) fires whenever local is detached and Jira has a parent — with no provenance check. It cannot distinguish 'we deliberately detached a parent we managed' from 'a human just set a parent we never managed'.

## Design (consumes the shared managed-ref gate from parent story safe-luge-nog)

Gate the parent CLEAR through the SAME `should_propagate_removal` provenance gate as links: emit the CLEAR only when the Jira parent ref is in `managed_refs` (we managed it, user detached) — otherwise ADOPT it inbound (the restored prior behavior). One shared pattern across parent + links.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The parent CLEAR is emitted ONLY when the Jira parent is in the ticket's `managed_refs` (a parent we managed, then detached).
- [ ] A human-set Jira parent on a locally-parentless, never-managed ticket is ADOPTED inbound, not cleared (regression test).
- [ ] A genuinely-detached managed parent still emits the CLEAR and converges (the drum-ruler-suit behavior preserved), incl. across a compaction boundary.
- [ ] Parent + link removal share the same gate helper (no duplicated provenance logic).
compiled_state.title: Parent CLEAR is un-gated: clobbers a human-set-in-Jira parent on a locally-parentless ticket
